subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV/FSSA, World Bank, EBRD, EC, RSF, TI, V-Dem, Freedom House) + Moldovan independent press + NBM data.

A genuine pro-EU reform democracy that proved resilient (the governing party held power and the EU referendum despite massive Russian interference; accession-negotiating), but Europe's poorest state under acute Russian-hybrid pressure.

Standout tension: EU-reform/accession upside vs an acute Russia/Transnistria destabilization tail and Europe's steepest demographic decline. Source-lean: MODERATE-HIGH Western framing (Russian-state sources excluded).

economic.notes: A reforming central bank and cleaned-up banks, on a small, poor, donor-dependent base.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt under ~45% GDP but a widening deficit and donor/EU dependence, with a new IMF arrangement. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: A credible, reforming central bank on inflation-targeting, with high reserve requirements hindering transmission. (IMF FSSA 2026; NBM)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The leu is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Eased from energy-shock peaks (~6.7%), re-accelerating on oil. (NBM)

banking_stability.notes: Cleaned up after the 2014 mega-fraud, with strong capital and low NPLs passing severe stress tests. (IMF FSSA 2026)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Wine/agriculture, textiles, and remittances, EU-reorienting after the loss of Russia/Transnistria gas - small, poor, and shock-prone. (IMF; EBRD)

institutional.notes: A real reform drive (justice-vetting, de-oligarchization) from a weak, corruption-dogged base.

rule_of_law.notes: Genuine justice-vetting, an anti-corruption court, and de-oligarchization, against a weak base and reform fatigue. (TI CPI 2025; EC; V-Dem)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Weak and thin, reforming amid brain drain. (IMF; EC)

press_freedom.notes: Free-ish with declining oligarch control, against Kremlin-disinformation saturation. (RSF 2025; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: Democratic resilience under acute pressure, against deep pro-EU/pro-Russia cleavages and demographic collapse.

political_stability.notes: The pro-EU government held power and the EU referendum despite massive Russian interference/vote-buying - resilience proven but acutely targeted. (Atlantic Council; OSCE)

civil_liberties.notes: Assembly, speech, and religion mostly protected and competitive. (Freedom House 2025)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A lawful regime that hosted Ukrainian refugees generously. (UNHCR)

social_cohesion.notes: A hard pro-EU/pro-Russia split with Gagauzia and Transnistria cleavages and Russian-funded grievance. (Crisis Group; Carnegie)

demographics.notes: Among Europe's steepest declines (down ~35% since 1991), with heavy emigration. (OSW; UNFPA)

geopolitical.notes: A fast EU-pivot, against an acute Russia/Transnistria-hybrid tail.

alliance_reliability.notes: A fast EU-pivot (negotiating accession), but militarily neutral, non-NATO, and weak. (EC)

conflict_involvement.notes: The frozen Transnistria conflict (Russian troops), war-adjacency, and being the prime target of Russian hybrid warfare and gas-weaponization - a real elevated tail. (ICG; Carnegie)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Open and EU-aligning, not sanctioned. (IMF; EC)

physical_practical.notes: Energy de-risked but a weak health system and agriculture-exposed climate.

climate.notes: An SE-Europe agrarian economy hit by intensifying drought/heat on rain-fed farming, with low readiness/resources. (ND-GAIN; WB CCDR)

healthcare.notes: Underfunded, with a severe doctor shortage from medic emigration. (WHO)

infrastructure.notes: Poor-to-moderate, energy de-risked from Russian gas post-2022 but still vulnerable. (IEA; EBRD)

crime_safety.notes: Moderate, with Transnistria grey-zone smuggling. (US State Dept)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low penetration against drought/seismic exposure. (World Bank)

summary: A genuine pro-EU reform democracy that proved resilient - the governing party held power and won the EU referendum despite massive Russian interference, and the country is now negotiating accession with a real justice-reform and de-oligarchization drive and cleaned-up banks. But it is Europe's poorest state under acute Russian-hybrid pressure: the frozen Transnistria conflict with Russian troops, gas-weaponization, deep pro-EU/pro-Russia social cleavages, and among Europe's steepest demographic declines. The outcome distribution is genuinely two-sided - EU-accession upside against a destabilization tail.
